title: Bouncy-QQ Custard Baobing
description: A kid-friendly Chinese dessert featuring a bouncy-qq custard and a crunchy namul-blanch-season topping.
instructions: Mix together the flour, butter, and sugar until a crumbly mixture forms.
Press the mixture into the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
Meanwhile, whisk together the eggs, milk, and salt in a separate bowl.
Add the cornstarch to the egg mixture and whisk until smooth.
Pour the egg mixture over the baked crust and smooth the top.
Bake for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the custard is set and lightly golden brown.
While the custard is baking, mix together the namul-blanch-season and shojin-friendly vegetable oil in a small bowl.
When the custard is done, sprinkle the namul-blanch-season mixture over the top and serve warm.
Let the Baobing cool to room temperature before serving.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve chilled, garnished with additional namul-blanch-season if desired.
